title: SuperMicro takes on server leaders as AMD pushes on-premise AI
summary: SuperMicro has seen significant growth in the server market, specifically in AI-related infrastructure, pushing past rivals like Lenovo and HPE to become the second-largest PC server maker behind Dell. This shift is occurring as AMD introduces "Agent Computers" designed for on-premise agentic AI workloads, aiming to compete with cloud-based AI solutions. The market is experiencing price volatility for components like GPUs, DRAM, and SSDs due to high demand, which is expected to continue impacting market dynamics in 2026.

title: AI-Driven Memory Chip Demand to Drive Micron's DRAM Revenue in Q2
summary: Micron Technology (MU) is expected to see significant growth in its second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue, driven by escalating demand for memory chips in AI applications. The company's DRAM segment, fueled by strong ties with tech giants like NVIDIA and AMD, is projected to be a major contributor, with estimated year-over-year growth of 145.5%. Micron's mass production of HBM3E memory for NVIDIA's AI chips further solidifies its position in the high-performance computing market.

title: Celestica, AMD partner for ‘Helios’ rack-scale AI platform
summary: Celestica Inc. (CLS) and AMD have formed a strategic partnership to launch the "Helios" rack-scale AI platform. This collaboration combines AMD's computing leadership with Celestica's expertise in networking switch technologies, focusing on the R&D, design, and manufacturing of scale-up networking switches for the platform. The "Helios" platform, based on Open Compute Project standards, is expected to be available to customers in late 2026, utilizing advanced networking silicon to interconnect next-generation AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs for large-scale AI clusters.

title: Lam Research (LCRX) Gained From Strong Demand Uptick
summary: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) was a leading contributor to Artisan Value Fund's outperformance, as highlighted in their fourth-quarter 2025 investor letter. The company, a leader in wafer fabrication equipment, is experiencing strong demand for its chipmaking tools due to the massive buildout of data centers running AI applications. Artisan Value Fund purchased LRCX shares when they were undervalued, recognizing the company's solid financial condition and attractive valuation.

title: Inside "Helios": Celestica and AMD build AI racks for cloud, enterprise by 2026
summary: Celestica (TSX: CLS) and AMD (NASDAQ: AMD) have announced a strategic collaboration to launch the "Helios" rack-scale AI platform, targeting cloud, enterprise, and research environments by late 2026. Celestica will be responsible for the R&D, design, and manufacturing of scale-up networking switches that are optimized for AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs and will utilize the UALoE architecture. The partnership aims to deliver high-performance AI infrastructure quickly and efficiently, leveraging both companies' expertise to meet the growing demand for large-scale AI deployments.

title: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) Is Up 5.4% After $200 Million AI Cloud Deal With Major Tech Client
summary: Akamai Technologies saw its stock rise by 5.4% following the announcement of a four-year, $200 million AI services agreement with a major U.S. tech company. This deal involves deploying one of the world's largest NVIDIA Blackwell RTX PRO 6000 Server Edition GPU clusters on its cloud platform, reinforcing Akamai's focus on cloud and AI growth. However, the company also faces internal debate regarding political spending transparency and potential risks like increased CapEx and customer concentration.

title: Intel Corporation Stock (ISIN: US4581401001) Consolidates Near $45 Amid AI Push and Foundry Challenges
summary: Intel Corporation's stock is consolidating around $45, reflecting a balance between optimism for its AI innovations and ongoing losses in its foundry segment. The company is forging strategic alliances in AI and 6G, with its foundry business projected to break even by 2027. Despite a strong recovery in 2025, analysts mostly rate the stock as "hold" due to stretched valuations and execution risks, though long-term growth is anticipated.

title: Comfort Systems USA, Inc. $FIX Shares Purchased by Granahan Investment Management LLC
summary: Granahan Investment Management LLC increased its stake in Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) by 35.2% during the third quarter, acquiring an additional 24,160 shares and bringing their total ownership to 92,772 shares valued at $76.55 million. This makes Comfort Systems USA the 6th largest position in Granahan's portfolio. title: 20,700 Shares in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM Purchased by First Trust Bank Ltd.
summary: First Trust Bank Ltd. recently acquired 20,700 shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) for approximately $5.78 million, making it their fourth-largest holding and 13.2% of their portfolio. The semiconductor giant commands about 70% of the global foundry market and recently reported strong quarterly results with a $1.75 trillion market cap. Analysts widely recommend a "Buy" rating for TSM, with a consensus price target of $391.43, despite current geopolitical risks affecting foreign markets.

title: A Look At Public Service Enterprise Group’s Valuation As It Moves To Eliminate Supermajority Voting Requirements
summary: Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) is proposing to eliminate supermajority voting requirements, a governance change that could impact shareholder value. The company's stock is currently trading at $83.73, considered modestly undervalued based on fair value estimates but slightly expensive by a Discounted Cash Flow model. Future demand for electricity, especially from data centers, and regulatory decisions on grid investment will be key factors influencing PEG's valuation.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Drops As Insiders Cash Out $33 Million
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong Q4 earnings, beating expectations with impressive revenue growth and a positive outlook for its data center segment. Despite institutional buying, the stock is trading below key moving averages, and insiders have sold approximately $33.1 million worth of shares. Competitive pressures from a new Chinese GPU maker and Meta's in-house chip development also pose potential headwinds for AMD.

title: Moody's economist Mark Zandi on thousands of layoffs at Jack Dorsey's Block; If companies cut workers as Block did, outright job losses appear to be ...
summary: Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi has raised concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence on the labor market following Jack Dorsey's Block's announcement of 4,000 job cuts. Although Block didn't explicitly blame AI, Zandi suggests that automation was a key factor and that increased AI adoption is linked to weaker hiring. He warns that if other companies follow Block's lead, especially given the market's positive reaction to the layoffs, outright job losses are increasingly likely.

title: Bank of Nova Scotia Sells 748,107 Shares of Intel Corporation $INTC

title: Should Flex’s Expanded US AMD Instinct AI Manufacturing Shift the Investment Narrative for Flex (FLEX)?
summary: Flex has expanded its collaboration with AMD to manufacture the AMD Instinct MI355X AI platform in the US, with production already underway in Austin, Texas. This development reinforces Flex's role in high-density AI hardware systems for data centers. While this strengthens Flex's position in the growing AI market and supports revenue guidance, investor concerns about customer concentration and potential insourcing by hyperscalers persist.

title: AMD and Celestica Announce Collaboration to Advance the Next Era of AI with “Helios” Rack-Scale AI Platform
summary: AMD and Celestica have announced a strategic collaboration to launch the "Helios" rack-scale AI platform, aimed at accelerating the deployment of AI infrastructure. This partnership combines AMD's expertise in high-performance and AI computing with Celestica's capabilities in advanced networking switch technologies. The "Helios" platform, based on Open Compute Project standards, will utilize advanced networking silicon to interconnect next-generation AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs, with availability expected in late 2026.

title: Diodes Inc Stock (ISIN: US2545431015) Holds Ground Amid Semiconductor Volatility—Key Catalysts Ahead
summary: Diodes Inc. (ISIN: US2545431015) is navigating a volatile semiconductor market, maintaining operational momentum despite broader industry headwinds. The company's diversified market exposure, conservative financials, and focus on high-reliability analog components position it well for long-term growth, particularly in the European automotive EV transition, industrial automation, and renewable energy sectors. Investors are closely monitoring margin trends and China exposure, while the stock offers potential for long-term investors despite expected near-term volatility.

title: Synopsys Software-defined Hardware-assisted Verification Enables AI Proliferation
summary: Synopsys has introduced significant advancements in its hardware-assisted verification (HAV) portfolio, including new hardware platforms and software-defined capabilities. These innovations aim to address the escalating complexity of AI chip verification, especially for multi-die and AI chips, by offering improved performance, scalability, and broader use case coverage. The company's ZeBu and HAPS platforms now support complex designs for data center AI, GPU, accelerators, and edge AI applications, leveraging AMD's processors and FPGAs to accelerate system bring-up and verification.

title: Super Micro Computer, Inc. Trade Ideas — XETR:MS51
summary: This article compiles various trading ideas and technical analyses for Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI), discussing both bearish and bullish outlooks. title: GLOBALFOUNDRIES (GFS) Is Down 10.7% After Mubadala’s $42 Secondary Sale And $300M Buyback Shift
summary: GLOBALFOUNDRIES (GFS) saw a 10.7% drop after its largest shareholder, Mubadala, completed a secondary offering of 20 million shares at $42 each. Concurrently, GlobalFoundries executed a $300 million share repurchase as part of its existing buyback authorization, shifting the company's ownership structure without altering its operational priorities. This move, alongside an expanded multi-billion dollar manufacturing partnership with Renesas, frames the company's immediate investment narrative focusing on demand stabilization and execution risks.

title: Intel Corporation Stock (ISIN: US4581401001) Faces Mixed Signals Amid Foundry Challenges and Institu
summary: Intel Corporation stock is experiencing mixed signals, trading around $45 as of March 15, 2026. This comes amidst institutional stake adjustments, concerns over Q1 guidance showing flat EPS expectations, and ongoing efforts to turnaround its foundry business to compete with rivals like TSMC and Samsung, targeting break-even margins by 2027. European investors are closely watching these developments, balancing the company's product launches and AI ambitions against governance risks from a shareholder lawsuit, geopolitical tensions, and an elevated P/E ratio despite profitability hurdles.

title: Qualcomm Inc Stock (ISIN: US7475251036) Falls to $130 as Memory Crisis Caps Growth—What Investors Should Know
summary: Qualcomm Inc. stock has fallen to $130, down 23% year-to-date, despite beating earnings, due to weak forward guidance caused by a global memory shortage. Insider selling and bearish analyst downgrades further signal concerns about muted sales and EPS growth, especially with Apple's potential move away from Qualcomm modems. The company faces challenges in its core mobile business, with diversification into automotive and data centers still being too small to offset current headwinds.

title: 15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors Rich
summary: This article highlights Ultra Clean Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:UCTT) as one of the AI stocks generating significant returns for investors. title: Qualcomm Inc Stock (ISIN: US7475251036) Falls to $130 as Memory Crisis Caps Growth—What Investors Should Know
summary: Qualcomm Inc. stock fell to $130 after an earnings beat was overshadowed by weak forward guidance due to a global memory shortage impacting smartphone chip demand. Investors are concerned about the company's reliance on the maturing smartphone market and the potential loss of Apple's modem business, despite diversification efforts into automotive and data center chips.

Intel's strategic pivot to foundry services and its focus on AI and client computing are key factors in its recovery efforts.

title: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust Sees Inflows Amid Tech Volatility

title: Intel Corporation Stock (ISIN: US4581401001) Faces Shareholder Lawsuit Amid Strong Recovery and Chip Shortage Tailwinds
summary: Intel Corporation is facing a shareholder lawsuit alleging the company granted a 10% equity stake to the U.S. government to mitigate political pressure. Despite this legal challenge, Intel's stock has shown strong recovery, fueled by new product launches, ongoing chip demand, and its foundry ambitions. The company's Q4 2025 results beat revenue estimates, but Q1 2026 guidance was cautious, reflecting its transition from legacy chips to AI and data center growth, supported by government subsidies and European expansion plans.
